title: Pool Maintenance Services vs DIY: A Comparison Guide
author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
excerpt: This guide compares professional pool maintenance services with DIY approaches, examining the differences in cost, quality, and time investment. Pool owners can make an informed decision based on their lifestyle and needs.
body:
Pool Maintenance: DIY vs Professional Services - A Comparison Guide

Maintaining your swimming pool can be time and energy-consuming. It may also cost more than the DIY route, so you need to decide your commitment level.

Unfortunately, according to Mr. Right, failing to maintain your pool on time can be harmful. Plus, it may not be a pleasing task to many pool owners.

Some individuals find cleaning their pool system therapeutic. Others prefer having a great time basking on their poolside and hiring professionals to help maintain it.

But which one is the best approach to keeping your pool maintained and operational? This is a common question asked by many pool owners. Fortunately, this comparison guide will discuss the benefits of hiring pool maintenance services vs those of a DIY approach.

Pool Maintenance Services vs DIY: Overview

Hiring a professional pool maintenance company offers you the best way to care for your swimming pool effectively.

Professionals can help clean your pool and keep it ready for use for a long time. As a result, you'll never worry about your pool having stained liners or dirty water.

Before hiring a skilled pool maintenance team, it's important to understand what exactly they do.

In most cases, their weekly or bi-weekly pool maintenance services will include:

  • Vacuuming and brushing pool surfaces
  • Skimming the pool water
  • Cleaning the pool's deck area and walls
  • Checking pool water pH
  • Monitoring the water level
  • Emptying the pool's skimmer baskets
  • Ensuring effective performance of the filters
  • Maintaining a proper balance of the pool chemicals
  • Shocking the pool

They may also help open your pool during spring and close it at the beginning of winter.

In short, hiring a reliable pool maintenance company makes you the boss. All you do is sit back and handle other essential tasks that require your attention.

You'll also be confident that the pool is clean and safe for you and your family.

Although opting for professional pool maintenance services can be more expensive for some pool owners, many owners believe that the cost is worth it for the peace of mind that it is being done right.

Pool Maintenance Services vs DIY: Comparison

These pool maintenance approaches are similar in that they all have the same goal: to keep a pool system clean, safe, and functional.

But the differences between them are important. Here's how pool maintenance services and DIY approaches differ:

Cost

The cost incurred is one of the key distinguishing factors between hiring a professional and doing the task yourself.

As stated above, a DIY approach can be a cost-efficient way to keep your pool operational. However, you'll still need to invest in good quality pool maintenance equipment such as:

  • Cleaning brush
  • Pool vacuum
  • Pool skimmer

Such equipment can be expensive, but you'll buy them only once.

Chemicals, filter cleaners, and water testing kits require frequent replacements. Therefore, they can be costly over time.

But when you hire an expert to look after your pool, you won't have to buy cleaning products, materials, and equipment.

Instead, you'll pay for the services rendered. The charges may vary from one company to another.

The average cost of cleaning a pool on a one-time depends on the level of algae or contamination present. If you plan to hire the cleaning service once or twice a week, the cost may range from $100 to $150 per month.

Here are the factors that may influence the cost of your professional pool service:

  • Pool size: Larger pools need a lot of chemicals and more time to clean than smaller pools.
  • Location: A pool situated next to trees, foliage, and a wooded area will require regular cleaning more than one in an open space. That's because it collects more debris. Besides, pools located in regions that experience heavy rainfall require regular maintenance.
  • Pool type: The cost of maintaining natural, chlorinated and salted pools vary.
  • Usage: A pool with regular usage will need more frequent maintenance than those used occasionally.
  • Pool equipment: The quality and efficiency of pool equipment will influence performance. Add-on features like lighting and heating will also need more service and repairs.

Quality

A professionally maintained pool will often look better than a DIY project.

That's because the company has trained experts in this industry, meaning they know how to clean your pool properly.

In addition, they use professional-grade equipment and chemicals to clean and keep your pool safe for use. However, the quality may vary depending on the experience of the company you choose.

Consider handling the pool maintenance yourself if you perfectly understand of the following:

  • How a pool functions
  • How to balance and maintain pool chemicals
  • How to clean the pool system
  • How to replace or clean the filters

If you know all these, you can effectively maintain your pool. But if you aren't familiar with them, don't risk it. Instead, hire a professional to help you with the task.

Time and Effort

Pool cleaning and maintenance can be time and effort-consuming. The process involves a lot of tasks like regular cleaning, opening and closing it accordingly, fixing any damage for proper functioning and improved lifespan.

While you may know how to do all these, you may not have enough time or energy to do so more often.

Hiring a professional will relieve you of the burden and stress of maintaining your pool. They use extensive experience and equipment to complete the task in the shortest time possible.

That means you'll have more time to enjoy your pool. A pool maintenance service differs from a DIY project in terms of cost, effort, and quality of the results.

There's no approach that's necessarily better than the other. But it's essential to consider one that suits your lifestyle and needs best.
